element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic; player can switch active character at any time during exploration and combat
Each weapon has distinct range/speed tradeoffs: sword (short-range broad strokes), foil (medium-range precision), bow (long-range but requires aiming until upgrade)
Acquired at narrative checkpoints rather than through player accomplishment; progression-locked rather than earned
Affect only active character; inactive party members are unaffected and continue following
Tag-team based; may require switching characters to solve
Zagato's minions encountered throughout narrative; all minions die (Ascot, Caldina, Rafarga) unlike manga/anime
Only character upgrade path independent of story gates; found by exploration
Automatic upgrades as part of level progression; not found or purchased individually

typical_relief_triggers: Narrative cutscenes with character journal entries
typical_tension_triggers: Encounters with faster/enhanced enemy AI (NA version localization)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Story-gated progression with tag-team combat breaks; narrative pacing punctuates action segments via character journals and dialogue

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden special items for HP/MP stat increases discoverable during exploration independent of main narrative path

difficulty_curve.early_game: Designed as accessible entry point with tag-team mechanic reducing party damage intake
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased difficulty via story-gated magic spell and character upgrade introductions
difficulty_curve.late_game: Culmination against Zagato with accumulated abilities and equipment upgrades
notable_spikes: NA localization increased overall difficulty: enhanced enemy AI and faster enemy movement compared to Japanese version
Boss encounters with Zagato's minions present escalating threats

notable_level_patterns.description: Puzzles solvable through switching between three characters; each character's unique weapon/ability set required to progress
Combat zones with hazards; hazards affect only active character, creating tactical switching opportunities
Narrative checkpoint automatically grants new magic spell or character upgrade; player cannot earn these independently
Off-path areas contain special items that permanently increase HP/MP; primary progression locked behind story gates
